verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein expressed specifically on mature sperm. It is restricted to the sperm cell surface (germline/gamete lineage) where it mediates sperm-oocyte fusion. It has its own TM anchor but its surface display is entirely restricted to the germline/reproductive lineage (spermatozoa), not expressed on somatic cells. This maps to `contextual` / `tissue_restricted_surface`: the protein has a bona fide TM domain and outer-leaflet ectodomain, but expression and surface display are confined to a single narrow germline lineage. HGNC gene-group membership in 'Gamete adhesion and fusion factors' and 'Ig-like cell adhesion molecule family' corroborates restricted surface display. Not a broadly expressed surface protein qualifying for `yes`.
